phase1.explanation:
2D arrays are arrays of arrays! Think of them as grids with rows and columns:

Declaration: type[][] name = {{row1}, {row2}, ...};

Access: name[row][col] — row then column

Rows: matrix.length
Columns: matrix[row].length

Nested loops: outer loop iterates rows, inner loop iterates columns

Jagged arrays: each row can have different lengths!

Common use cases:
• Matrices (math operations)
• Game boards (tic-tac-toe, chess)
• Tabular data (spreadsheets)

Pro tip: Always use nested for loops to traverse 2D arrays!

phase3.prompt:
Create a 2x3 matrix (2 rows, 3 columns) with values:
Row 1: 1, 2, 3
Row 2: 4, 5, 6

Print the matrix in a grid format.
dialogueHints: Use nested for loops: outer for rows, inner for columns
Use matrix[row].length to get column count
phase3.openingDialogue: Let's go 2D! Grids and matrices, oh my~ 🎯
